---
title: Policies
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
---

This page is under construction.

## Compute

1. Personal Health Information (PHI) data is __NOT__ permitted on the HPC Cluster.
1. Coordinate with the ND-HNC group when utilizing a large portion of the compute resources (e.g. $>$ 400 CPUs on the HPC cluster).
    - This helps to ensure that all deadlines can be met (e.g. first-year or M.Sc. projects, revise-and-resubmit).


## Storage

1. Storage of Personal Health Information (PHI) data is __NOT__ permitted.
1. Users or groups using a disproportionate amount of resources will be required to reduce their footprint or  ___risk losing access___.
1. Intermediate Data can be regenerated from Original Data and should not be stored in Cold or Active Storage.
1. Data stored in Scratch should be for active analyses only.
1. Storing third-party data (e.g. HCP, NKI, ABCD) is not permitted on Cold or Active Storage.
    - Only the Final Data should be maintained.

<!--
## Schedule

Scheduling
Billing

## Level 2-4

Level 2
Safety training
Safety test
Annual renewal
Annual screener
SOP Safety

Level 3
Level 2 reqs
First Aid
CPR/AED
Train: un/load participant
SOP Data

Level 4
Level 2, 3 reqs
Shadow hours
Test: run scanner, trouble shoot reconstruct, data export, emergency response

## Computers

Organize Stimulus Control
Organize DICOM Computer

## Trainings

Regular Safety, SOP, Medical trainings -->
